seed_instruction: Write a email to your professor to ask for an extention on a project due in two days.
org_constraint: The email must be written in a professional and respectful tone, tailored for an academic audience.
new_constraint: Write the email in an informal tone, tailored for a casual audience.
instruction: Write a email to your professor to ask for an extention on a project due in two days. The email must be written in a professional and respectful tone, tailored for an academic audience. Write the email in an informal tone, tailored for a casual audience.
response:
Here are both versions of the email, tailored to your specifications:

***

## 🧑‍🏫 Professional and Respectful Tone (Academic Audience)

**Subject:** Extension Request - [Course Name] - [Project Name]

Dear Professor [Professor's Last Name],

I hope this email finds you well.

I am writing to respectfully request a short extension on the [Project Name] assignment, which is currently due on [Date, e.g., Wednesday, October 25th].

I have been making good progress on the project, but due to [**brief, professional reason - e.g., a heavier-than-anticipated workload this week, a minor unforeseen personal commitment, etc.**], I find that I need a little more time to ensure the quality of my submission meets my standards and your expectations.

Would it be possible to receive an extension until [Suggest a specific new date, e.g., Friday, October 27th]?

I apologize for any inconvenience this request may cause and I appreciate you considering my situation. I am committed to submitting high-quality work and am confident I can deliver it by the requested date.

Thank you for your time and understanding.

Sincerely,

[Your Name]
[Your Student ID Number]

***

## 🥳 Informal Tone (Casual Audience)

**Subject:** Quick Q about the [Project Name] deadline 🤔

Hey Professor [Professor's Last Name],

Hope you're having a good week!

Quick question about the [Project Name]—it's due in two days, and I was wondering if there was any chance I could get a small extension?

Things have been a little crazy/busy lately with [**brief, casual reason - e.g., a few unexpected things popping up, just needing a bit more time to really nail the research, etc.**], and I want to make sure I hand in something I'm actually proud of, not just something rushed. 😅

Would it be okay if I turned it in on [Suggest a specific new date, e.g., Friday] instead?

So sorry to even ask! Let me know if that works or if there’s another way I should handle this.

Thanks so much!

Best,

[Your Name]<turn|>
